summary: Deliver the next M1 C1 editor slice: context-aware completion for the version-labeled provisional OpenSCAD 2021.01 built-in corpus and lexically visible current-file declarations, completion-list signatures and paraphrased help, themed CodeMirror completion surfaces, namespace-safe user shadowing, and a deterministic provisional cube call skeleton. Q-0013, Q-0014, and Q-0017 continue to bound the final exhaustive corpus, cross-file adapter, insertion policy, and persistent call-site-help claims.
specSections: Section 2.7 questions, decisions, and unit of work
Section 4.1 C1 code editor with OpenSCAD language support
Section 4.1 FR-1.3 built-in completion and signature hints
Section 8 M1 C1 excluding diff view

externalInputs.purpose: Use the official OpenSCAD documentation index as the permitted route to language-reference material.
Confirm the public v2021.01 built-in module, function, special-form, and special-variable corpus and representative signatures.
Cross-check release-tag primitive parameter names and defaults rather than treating call-tip examples as defaults.
Cross-check the official 2021.01 built-in function registrations and callable signatures.
Confirm the official 2021.01 import parameter order and the origin, scale, and convexity defaults.
Use the official Completion, CompletionContext, CompletionSource, language-data, and completion-theme integration contracts.
Resolve and pin the approved direct autocomplete runtime dependency at version 6.20.3.

decisions.decision: Keep the existing `openscad-2021.01-official-reference` name corpus as the completion pin, add all eleven documented special variables, keep signatures in neutral JSON language data, and keep paraphrased visible descriptions in the English message catalog.
decisions.rationale: One shared version label prevents the live manual from silently changing behavior; separating code-like signatures from visible prose satisfies language-data reuse, localization, and the component color policy without weakening any gate.

decisions.decision: Use the authored Lezer tree plus a lightweight lexical-state pass to suppress completion inside closed or unfinished comments, strings, include/use paths, callable names, and parameter-name declarations; separate statement and expression candidates; and use a small preceding-token fallback only where an incomplete tree has no decisive ancestor.
decisions.rationale: Syntax context avoids offering modules where values are required, while the lexical-state pass covers unfinished constructs that intentionally have no closed syntax node yet.

decisions.decision: Extract document declarations plus the cursor's lexical chain of module/function parameters, block declarations, and for/let bindings from the current incremental syntax tree; retain callable parameter text; keep function, module, and variable namespaces distinct; and let a same-namespace user declaration replace its built-in completion.
decisions.rationale: This delivers the unblocked current-file half of FR-1.4 without inventing the C6 project-source adapter, leaking nested declarations outside their scope, or hiding a function merely because a variable shares its label.

decisions.decision: Offer `cube(size = 1, center = false)` as the completion detail and provisionally replace `cub` with `cube(size = 1, center = false);` when accepted.
decisions.rationale: The source is valid, deterministic, explicit about defaults, and directly exercises AC-1.b while Q-0013 continues to reserve the owner-final named-versus-positional insertion policy.

decisions.decision: Register the source through OpenSCAD language data so the existing keyboard-accessible CodeMirror completion machinery owns interaction, verify list selection and Enter acceptance in a live EditorView, and style its list, selection, details, and info surfaces exclusively with Appendix C-derived CSS variables.
decisions.rationale: The editor retains standard completion keyboard behavior and live theme switching without a parallel popup implementation or hardcoded presentation colors.

testEvidence.criterion: FR-1.3 and AC-1.b built-in completion foundation
testEvidence.test: The OpenSCAD language source offers `cube` for `cub`, exposes signature and paraphrased help, supplies the provisional deterministic insertion text, covers every name in the provisional corpus, and is registered with live language support.

red.outcome: RED: suite failed before test collection because the required openscad-completion module did not exist.

green.outcome: GREEN: 3 files and 21 tests passed, including catalog integrity, provisional acceptance text, live language-source registration, and Enter-key acceptance in an EditorView.

testEvidence.test: Completion distinguishes statement, expression, and special-variable positions, handles an expression after let bindings, removes display whitespace, and preserves release-accurate representative defaults.

red.outcome: RED: 3 of 6 tests failed; let-expression completion returned modules, one signature had trailing whitespace, and linear_extrude/text defaults were inaccurate.

green.outcome: GREEN: 3 files and 21 tests passed after syntax-tree context selection, unfinished-region suppression, and official-release default corrections.

testEvidence.test: Current-file modules, functions, variables, parameters, block locals, and for/let bindings appear only in valid lexical contexts; callable signatures preserve parameters; user modules replace same-name built-ins; and nested assignments do not leak to top level.

red.outcome: RED: 2 of 9 tests failed because current-file declarations were absent and a user-defined cube did not replace the built-in candidate.

green.outcome: GREEN: 3 files and 21 tests passed with lexical-scope current-file symbol extraction and same-namespace shadowing.

testEvidence.test: A variable sharing the `sin` label remains a variable candidate without deleting the built-in function, and built-in spellings are valid user variable-binding identifiers in the grammar.

red.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/ui/editor/openscad-completion.test.ts -t "keeps function and variable namespaces distinct" --reporter=verbose
red.outcome: RED: the single focused test failed because only one `sin` candidate remained; the grammar had not parsed the built-in spelling as an assignment declaration.

green.outcome: GREEN: 3 files and 21 tests passed after binding-position grammar support, generated-parser refresh, and namespace-keyed deduplication.

testEvidence.test: Unfinished comments, strings, and include paths stay silent; simple and nested-default declaration parameter names stay silent; let is present in both valid forms; current lexical scopes are complete; and corrected import/parent_module/polygon/polyhedron signatures retain release defaults.

red.outcome: RED: 4 of 11 tests failed; let was absent, unfinished block comments leaked candidates, four signatures were incomplete, and parameters/locals/iterator bindings were missing from lexical scope.

green.outcome: GREEN: 3 files and 21 tests passed; independent adversarial re-review also passed 14 targeted contexts with no remaining release blocker.

testEvidence.test: The complete repository unit and integration suite remains green after completion, grammar, scoped-symbol, message-catalog, and theme changes.

red.outcome: RED: the new completion slice could not collect tests because its product module did not exist.

green.outcome: GREEN: all 30 test files and 288 tests passed; the subsequent production build, lint/source-policy gate, and 3-test E2E lane also passed.

questions: Q-0013 remains open: the v2021.01 catalog and named-argument cube insertion are explicitly provisional, not an owner-final exhaustive corpus or skeleton policy.
Q-0014 remains open: this slice analyzes the current buffer's lexical scopes and does not invent the include/use project-source adapter owned with C6.
Q-0017 was appended under Section 2.7: completion-list signatures proceed, while persistent active-parameter call-site help remains parked pending its required behavior.

The in-app browser rejected fresh tabs from its active session during the completion-popup QA attempt; no alternate browser-control surface was used, so rendered popup appearance remains unverified in this slice while automated integration and theme-policy checks continue.

Q-0001 remains open: the final Rust license-policy run is expected to fail exactly the previously recorded Unicode-3.0 transitive package records; no dependency or policy is changed to conceal that block.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
